# Problem

RFC 9457 problem-details object. The optional `code` field is a plexsphere extension that carries the machine-readable error taxonomy (RFC 8628 device-flow codes, federation_kind_mismatch, etc.). 

## Properties

Name | Type | Description | Notes
------------ | ------------- | ------------- | -------------
**type** | **str** | URI reference that identifies the problem type. | 
**title** | **str** | Short, human-readable summary of the problem. | 
**status** | **int** | HTTP status code generated for this occurrence. | 
**detail** | **str** | Human-readable explanation specific to this occurrence. | 
**instance** | **str** | URI reference identifying the specific occurrence. | 
**code** | **str** | Optional machine-readable error code. Device-flow errors use the RFC 8628 taxonomy (&#x60;authorization_pending&#x60;, &#x60;slow_down&#x60;, &#x60;access_denied&#x60;, &#x60;expired_token&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_grant&#x60;); service token mismatches use &#x60;federation_kind_mismatch&#x60;.  Tenancy Domain CRUD adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service sentinel and one HTTP status — see &#x60;docs/reference/platform/http.md#tenancy-domain-crud-px-0026&#x60;:    * &#x60;domain_slug_conflict&#x60; — 409, CreateDomain duplicate slug.   * &#x60;mesh_cidr_overlap&#x60; — 409, mesh_cidr collides with another Domain (Create / Patch).   * &#x60;invalid_domain&#x60; — 400, aggregate invariant rejected the body.   * &#x60;invalid_domain_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;domain_not_found&#x60; — 404, the addressed Domain does not exist or is not visible.   * &#x60;slug_immutable&#x60; — 400, &#x60;PatchDomain&#x60; body carried a &#x60;slug&#x60; field.   * &#x60;empty_patch&#x60; — 400, &#x60;PatchDomain&#x60; body had no mutable fields set.   * &#x60;invalid_reachability_policy&#x60; — 400, &#x60;reachability&#x60; partial fill (a non-zero policy must set every threshold).   * &#x60;mesh_cidr_invalidates_subrange&#x60; — 422, mesh_cidr retarget would orphan an existing project sub-range.   * &#x60;domain_not_empty&#x60; — 409, &#x60;DeleteDomain&#x60; blocked by remaining child aggregates (see &#x60;DomainChildCounts&#x60;).   * &#x60;permission_denied&#x60; — 403, ReBAC denied the operation. Emitted via the dedicated &#x60;PermissionDenied&#x60; schema (Story 8 deviation note).   * &#x60;unauthenticated&#x60; — 401, no resolved principal on the request.   * &#x60;invalid_limit&#x60; — 400, &#x60;ListDomains&#x60; &#x60;limit&#x60; query parameter was outside &#x60;[1, 200]&#x60;.   * &#x60;invalid_cursor&#x60; — 400, &#x60;ListDomains&#x60; opaque cursor was tampered or malformed (HMAC mismatch).   * &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60; — 403, &#x60;ListDomains&#x60; cursor was minted for a different caller than the one presenting it (per-(caller, pepper) HMAC binding rejected the replay).   * &#x60;invalid_body&#x60; — 400, request body was not a valid &#x60;DomainCreateRequest&#x60; / &#x60;DomainPatchRequest&#x60; JSON document.   * &#x60;request_body_too_large&#x60; — 413, request body exceeded the 8 KiB tenancy ceiling.  Tenancy Project CRUD adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;project_slug_conflict&#x60; — 409, CreateProject duplicate (domain_id, slug).   * &#x60;sub_range_overlap&#x60; — 409, sub_range_cidr overlaps a sibling Project&#39;s reservation.   * &#x60;parent_domain_missing&#x60; — 409, FK violation on Create — the parent Domain does not exist.   * &#x60;invalid_project&#x60; — 400, aggregate invariant rejected the body.   * &#x60;invalid_project_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;invalid_domain_filter&#x60; — 400, ListProjects &#x60;domain_id&#x60; query parameter was malformed.   * &#x60;project_not_found&#x60; — 404, the addressed Project does not exist or is not visible.   * &#x60;sub_range_invalidates_allocation&#x60; — 422, sub_range patch would invalidate an existing allocation (or contradicts itself with &#x60;release_sub_range&#x60;).   * &#x60;project_not_empty&#x60; — 409, DeleteProject blocked by remaining child aggregates (see &#x60;ProjectChildCounts&#x60;).   * &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60; — 403, &#x60;ListProjects&#x60; cursor was minted for a different caller than the one presenting it (per-(caller, pepper) HMAC binding rejected the replay).  Tenancy Identity listing + Invitations adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;invalid_kind&#x60; — 400, &#x60;kind&#x60; query parameter was not &#x60;user&#x60; or &#x60;service-identity&#x60;.   * &#x60;invalid_principal_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{principalId}&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;identity_not_found&#x60; — 404, principal does not exist or is in a different Domain.   * &#x60;invalid_status&#x60; — 400, &#x60;status&#x60; query parameter was not in {pending, accepted, revoked, expired, all}.   * &#x60;invalid_invitation_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{invitationId}&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;invalid_ttl&#x60; — 400, &#x60;ttl_seconds&#x60; was outside [60, 604800].   * &#x60;too_many_initial_tuples&#x60; — 422, &#x60;initial_tuples&#x60; exceeded the 32-entry cap.   * &#x60;invitation_object_out_of_scope&#x60; — 422, an &#x60;initial_tuples[].object&#x60; was not &#x60;domain:&lt;this-id&gt;&#x60; / &#x60;project:&lt;uuid&gt;&#x60; / &#x60;group:&lt;uuid&gt;&#x60;.   * &#x60;invalid_caveat_context&#x60; — 422, an &#x60;initial_tuples[].caveat_context&#x60; was not JSON-encodable losslessly.   * &#x60;invitation_already_pending&#x60; — 409, a pending invitation already exists for &#x60;(domain_id, external_subject)&#x60;.   * &#x60;invitation_already_accepted&#x60; — 409, target invitation has already been accepted (Revoke / Accept races).   * &#x60;invitation_already_expired&#x60; — 409, target invitation has already been expired.   * &#x60;invitation_not_found&#x60; — 404, invitation does not exist or is in a different Domain.   * &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60; — 403, &#x60;ListIdentities&#x60; / &#x60;ListInvitations&#x60; cursor was minted for a different caller than the one presenting it (per-(caller, pepper) HMAC binding rejected the replay).  CSRF defence-in-depth adds three 403 codes scoped to state-changing cookie-authenticated &#x60;/v1/*&#x60; requests. Bearer-authenticated requests and &#x60;GET&#x60; / &#x60;HEAD&#x60; requests are exempt. The codes are:    * &#x60;csrf-token-mismatch&#x60; — 403, the &#x60;X-Plexsphere-CSRF&#x60;     header is absent, empty, or does not byte-match the     &#x60;plexsphere_csrf&#x60; cookie. The cookie is rotated on     sign-in and on sign-out so a leaked token cannot     outlive the session it was minted for.   * &#x60;csrf-origin-mismatch&#x60; — 403, the request carried an     &#x60;Origin&#x60; header that is not in the configured     allowlist AND did not advertise     &#x60;Sec-Fetch-Site: same-origin&#x60;.   * &#x60;csrf-origin-not-configured&#x60; — 403, the deployment     does not configure &#x60;PLEXSPHERE_CSRF_ALLOWED_ORIGINS&#x60;.     A misconfigured production deployment surfaces this     code on every cookie-authenticated mutation rather     than silently bypassing the CSRF check.  Cloud Inventory CRUD adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service / validator sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;cloud_slug_conflict&#x60; — 409, CreateCloud duplicate slug.   * &#x60;cloud_external_id_conflict&#x60; — 409, CreateCloud duplicate &#x60;(provider, external_id)&#x60; pair.   * &#x60;invalid_cloud&#x60; — 400, Cloud aggregate invariant rejected the body.   * &#x60;invalid_cloud_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;cloud_not_found&#x60; — 404, the addressed Cloud does not exist or is not visible.   * &#x60;slug_immutable&#x60; — 400, &#x60;PatchCloud&#x60; (or &#x60;PatchDomain&#x60;) body carried a &#x60;slug&#x60; field. Shared with the tenancy taxonomy because the immutability semantics match.   * &#x60;provider_immutable&#x60; — 400, &#x60;PatchCloud&#x60; body carried a &#x60;provider&#x60; field — provider is the validator-routing key and changing it would invalidate every previously-stored endpoint blob.   * &#x60;unknown_provider&#x60; — 400, &#x60;provider&#x60; is not one of the supported closed-enum values (aws, azure).   * &#x60;invalid_cloud_endpoint&#x60; — 400, per-provider validator rejected one or more fields in the &#x60;endpoint&#x60; JSONB blob.   * &#x60;invalid_cloud_region_defaults&#x60; — 400, per-provider validator rejected one or more fields in the &#x60;region_defaults&#x60; JSONB blob.   * &#x60;cloud_not_empty&#x60; — 409, &#x60;DeleteCloud&#x60; blocked by at least one persisted &#x60;CloudCredential&#x60; (see &#x60;CloudChildCounts&#x60;).   * &#x60;clouds_not_provisioned&#x60; — 501, the composition root has not wired the Cloud Inventory dependency bundle yet.   * &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60; — 403, &#x60;ListClouds&#x60; cursor was minted for a different caller than the one presenting it (per-(caller, pepper) HMAC binding rejected the replay). Mirrors the &#x60;ListNodes&#x60; &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60; on &#x60;/v1/nodes&#x60;.  The operator-facing Cloud Credentials read + revoke surface adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service / read-service sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;invalid_cloud_credential_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; on &#x60;/v1/cloud-credentials/{id}&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;invalid_revoke_reason&#x60; — 400, &#x60;RevokeCloudCredential&#x60; body &#x60;reason&#x60; was empty or whitespace-only.   * &#x60;cloud_credential_not_found&#x60; — 404, the addressed Cloud Credential does not exist.   * &#x60;cloud_credentials_not_provisioned&#x60; — 501, the composition root has not wired the Cloud Credentials read + revoke dependency bundle yet.  The Cloud Credentials surface REUSES the shared &#x60;invalid_limit&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_cursor&#x60;, &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_body&#x60;, &#x60;request_body_too_large&#x60;, &#x60;unauthenticated&#x60;, and &#x60;internal&#x60; codes with the same semantics they carry on the Cloud Inventory surface above.  The operator-facing OpenBao Credential Broker read + lifecycle surface adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service / read-service sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;invalid_project_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; on &#x60;/v1/projects/{id}/credentials&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;invalid_credential_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; on &#x60;/v1/credentials/{id}&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;invalid_revoke_reason&#x60; — 400, &#x60;RevokeCredential&#x60; body &#x60;reason&#x60; was empty or whitespace-only.   * &#x60;invalid_rotate_material&#x60; — 400, &#x60;RotateCredential&#x60; body carried an empty payload, non-positive ttl, or otherwise malformed material.   * &#x60;credential_not_found&#x60; — 404, the addressed credential does not exist.   * &#x60;credential_cas_conflict&#x60; — 409, &#x60;RotateCredential&#x60; &#x60;expected_version&#x60; did not match the live broker-row version.   * &#x60;credential_revoked&#x60; — 409, &#x60;RotateCredential&#x60; targeted an already-revoked credential.   * &#x60;credentials_not_provisioned&#x60; — 501, the composition root has not wired the Credential Broker read + lifecycle dependency bundle yet.  The Credential Broker surface REUSES the shared &#x60;invalid_limit&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_cursor&#x60;, &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_body&#x60;, &#x60;request_body_too_large&#x60;, &#x60;unauthenticated&#x60;, and &#x60;internal&#x60; codes with the same semantics they carry on the Cloud Inventory surface above.  The Credential Assignment request + decision surface adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service / read-service sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;invalid_credential_assignment_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; on &#x60;/v1/credential-assignments/{id}/...&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;invalid_cloud_credential_id&#x60; — 400, &#x60;RequestCredentialAssignment&#x60; body &#x60;cloud_credential_id&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID. Reuses the same code string the Cloud Credentials path id carries.   * &#x60;invalid_decision_reason&#x60; — 400, &#x60;RejectCredentialAssignment&#x60; or &#x60;RevokeCredentialAssignment&#x60; body &#x60;reason&#x60; was empty or whitespace-only.   * &#x60;credential_assignment_not_found&#x60; — 404, the addressed Credential Assignment does not exist.   * &#x60;duplicate_live_assignment&#x60; — 409, a live Credential Assignment already exists for the same (Project, Cloud Credential) pair.   * &#x60;credential_not_assignable&#x60; — 422, the named Cloud Credential is not in an assignable lifecycle state.   * &#x60;illegal_transition&#x60; — 409, the requested lifecycle transition is not legal from the assignment&#39;s current state.   * &#x60;self_approval_denied&#x60; — 403, the caller may not approve a Credential Assignment they themselves requested.   * &#x60;credential_assignments_not_provisioned&#x60; — 501, the composition root has not wired the Credential Assignment dependency bundle yet.  The Credential Assignment surface REUSES the shared &#x60;invalid_project_id&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_limit&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_cursor&#x60;, &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_body&#x60;, &#x60;request_body_too_large&#x60;, &#x60;unauthenticated&#x60;, and &#x60;internal&#x60; codes with the same semantics they carry on the Cloud Inventory surface above.  The Approval Workflow dual-control surface adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;invalid_approval_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; on &#x60;/v1/approvals/{id}&#x60;, &#x60;.../approve&#x60;, &#x60;.../reject&#x60;, or &#x60;.../break-glass&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;invalid_break_glass_reason&#x60; — 400, &#x60;BreakGlassApproval&#x60; body &#x60;reason&#x60; was shorter than the 16-character emergency-justification floor or whitespace-only.   * &#x60;approval_not_found&#x60; — 404, the addressed Approval does not exist or is not visible.  The Approval Workflow surface REUSES the &#x60;invalid_decision_reason&#x60; (&#x60;RejectApproval&#x60; body &#x60;reason&#x60; was empty or whitespace-only), &#x60;self_approval_denied&#x60; (the caller may not approve a proposal they themselves raised), and &#x60;illegal_transition&#x60; (the requested lifecycle transition is not legal from the proposal&#39;s current state) code strings with the same semantics they carry on the Credential Assignment surface above, plus the shared &#x60;invalid_limit&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_cursor&#x60;, &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_body&#x60;, &#x60;unauthenticated&#x60;, &#x60;permission_denied&#x60;, and &#x60;internal&#x60; codes with the same semantics they carry on the surfaces above.  The read-only Blueprint Catalog surface adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / read-service sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;invalid_blueprint_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; on &#x60;/v1/blueprints/{id}&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;blueprint_not_found&#x60; — 404, the addressed Blueprint does not exist or is not visible.   * &#x60;blueprints_not_provisioned&#x60; — 501, the composition root has not wired the Blueprint Catalog read dependency bundle yet.  The Blueprint Catalog surface REUSES the shared &#x60;invalid_limit&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_cursor&#x60;, &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60;, &#x60;unauthenticated&#x60;, and &#x60;internal&#x60; codes with the same semantics they carry on the Cloud Inventory surface above.  The Tenancy Resource surface adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;invalid_resource_origin&#x60; — 400, &#x60;CreateResource&#x60; body &#x60;origin&#x60; was absent or not one of &#x60;adopted&#x60; / &#x60;provisioned&#x60;.   * &#x60;invalid_resource_id&#x60; — 400, path &#x60;{id}&#x60; on &#x60;/v1/resources/{id}&#x60; was not a non-zero UUID.   * &#x60;resource_not_found&#x60; — 404, the addressed Resource does not exist or is not visible.   * &#x60;resources_not_provisioned&#x60; — 501, the composition root has not wired the Resource dependency bundle yet.  The Tenancy Resource surface REUSES the shared &#x60;invalid_project_id&#x60;, &#x60;project_not_found&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_body&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_limit&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_cursor&#x60;, &#x60;cursor_binding_mismatch&#x60;, &#x60;request_body_too_large&#x60;, &#x60;unauthenticated&#x60;, and &#x60;internal&#x60; codes with the same semantics they carry on the Cloud Inventory and Tenancy surfaces above.  The Bridge Orchestrator surface adds the closed taxonomy below. Each code maps to exactly one repo / service / domain sentinel and one HTTP status:    * &#x60;resource_not_bridge&#x60; — 409, the addressed Resource exists but is not of kind &#x60;bridge&#x60;; emitted by every mutating bridge operation before any persistence write.   * &#x60;relay_port_out_of_range&#x60; — 400, a &#x60;listen_port&#x60; (relay, user-access provider) or &#x60;remote_port&#x60; (site-to-site tunnel) was outside the closed range &#x60;1..65535&#x60;.   * &#x60;slug_conflict&#x60; — 409, a many-aggregate create or patch collided on &#x60;(resource_id, slug)&#x60; (user-access, ingress, site-to-site) or on &#x60;(resource_id, sni_host)&#x60; (ingress).   * &#x60;target_node_not_in_domain&#x60; — 400, an ingress rule&#39;s &#x60;target_node_id&#x60; resolves to a Node outside the bridge Resource&#39;s owning Domain.   * &#x60;allowed_subnet_empty&#x60; — 400, a site-to-site tunnel create or patch supplied an empty &#x60;allowed_subnets&#x60; list.   * &#x60;secret_ref_malformed&#x60; — 400, an &#x60;auth_secret_ref&#x60; (user-access, site-to-site) did not match the opaque &#x60;secret:&lt;domain&gt;/&lt;project&gt;/&lt;name&gt;(:&lt;version&gt;)?&#x60; form.  The Bridge Orchestrator surface REUSES the shared &#x60;invalid_project_id&#x60;, &#x60;project_not_found&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_resource_id&#x60;, &#x60;resource_not_found&#x60;, &#x60;invalid_body&#x60;, &#x60;request_body_too_large&#x60;, &#x60;permission_denied&#x60;, &#x60;unauthenticated&#x60;, and &#x60;internal&#x60; codes with the same semantics they carry on the Tenancy Resource surface above.  Dependency-degradation codes are global rather than scoped to a single surface: any operation can return &#x60;503&#x60; when a dependency it needs is briefly unavailable, instead of a generic &#x60;5xx&#x60;, so a client branches on &#x60;code&#x60; to back off, fail over, or page rather than string-matching opaque internal errors. Each code maps to one dependency outage and one HTTP status; the operator runbook is &#x60;docs/operations/failure-modes.md&#x60;:    * &#x60;authz_unavailable&#x60; — 503, the ReBAC backend (SpiceDB) is     unreachable so an authorization decision could not be     reached. Emitted GLOBALLY by every ReBAC-gated operation —     no handler fails open. Within the per-Domain stale-check     TTL a last-known-good allow/deny may be served instead     (recorded under a distinct audit reason); past the TTL, or     for a subject not in the stale cache, the operation     returns this code.   * &#x60;event_stream_unavailable&#x60; — 503, &#x60;GetNodeEvents&#x60; could     not open the signed event stream because the message bus     (NATS JetStream) is unreachable. The reconciliation-pull     &#x60;GetNodeState&#x60; path does not depend on the message bus and     stays available during the same outage.   * &#x60;datastore_unavailable&#x60; — 503, &#x60;GetNodeState&#x60; could not     serve a snapshot because the datastore (Postgres) is     unreachable AND no fresh-enough cached snapshot exists for     the addressed Node. A Node with a fresh-enough cached     snapshot is still served &#x60;200&#x60; during the same outage.   * &#x60;signing_unavailable&#x60; — 503, &#x60;IssueSession&#x60; could not mint     a session token because the Signing Service is     unreachable. No Session is persisted and no token issued,     so a retry after recovery is safe.   * &#x60;openbao_unavailable&#x60; — 503, a node-secret read could not     complete because the secret backend (OpenBao) is sealed or     unreachable. DIVERGENCE NOTE: the project README&#39;s     degradation table uses the illustrative string     &#x60;secret-backend-unavailable&#x60; for this case, but the     shipped wire code is &#x60;openbao_unavailable&#x60;. The wire code     is authoritative — renaming it would be a breaking change     for zero behavioural gain — so the divergence is     documented here rather than reconciled.  None of the dependency-degradation codes carry the underlying error text on the wire: the cause is logged in the server&#39;s structured record only, and the Problem &#x60;detail&#x60; is a fixed, generic, retry-oriented string.  | [optional] 
**child_counts** | [**DomainChildCounts**](DomainChildCounts.md) | Optional structured payload set EXCLUSIVELY by the &#x60;domain_not_empty&#x60; problem (&#x60;DELETE /v1/domains/{id}&#x60; blocked by remaining child aggregates). Other problem instances omit the field. The generated client should treat its presence as a strong signal the response code is &#x60;domain_not_empty&#x60;.  | [optional] 
**project_child_counts** | [**ProjectChildCounts**](ProjectChildCounts.md) | Optional structured payload set EXCLUSIVELY by the &#x60;project_not_empty&#x60; problem (&#x60;DELETE /v1/projects/{id}&#x60; blocked by remaining child aggregates). Other problem instances omit the field. The generated client should treat its presence as a strong signal the response code is &#x60;project_not_empty&#x60;.  | [optional] 
**cloud_child_counts** | [**CloudChildCounts**](CloudChildCounts.md) | Optional structured payload set EXCLUSIVELY by the &#x60;cloud_not_empty&#x60; problem (&#x60;DELETE /v1/clouds/{id}&#x60; blocked by remaining child aggregates). Other problem instances omit the field. The generated client should treat its presence as a strong signal the response code is &#x60;cloud_not_empty&#x60;.  | [optional] 

## Example

```python
from plexsphere.models.problem import Problem

# TODO update the JSON string below
json = "{}"
# create an instance of Problem from a JSON string
problem_instance = Problem.from_json(json)
# print the JSON string representation of the object
print(Problem.to_json())

# convert the object into a dict
problem_dict = problem_instance.to_dict()
# create an instance of Problem from a dict
problem_from_dict = Problem.from_dict(problem_dict)
```
